title: Neko Rooms
description: Self-hosted room management system for n.eko virtual browsers, a rabb.it alternative for collaborative co-browsing and watch-together sessions.
installation: Neko Rooms spins up and tears down n.eko (virtual browser) containers on demand and exposes a management UI for them. It keeps no SQL database: room metadata and persistent room storage live on disk, so the bundled volume is mounted at /data (set NEKO_ROOMS_STORAGE_INTERNAL/EXTERNAL there) and no add-on is required. IMPORTANT runtime caveat: neko-rooms is built around the Docker host model and orchestrates SIBLING containers via the Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock) plus a WebRTC UDP port range (NEKO_ROOMS_EPR, default 59000-59049) and NAT1TO1 host IP. A vanilla Kubernetes pod has no Docker socket and cannot bind that wide UDP range, so to actually launch rooms this must run with access to a Docker/containerd daemon (e.g. a node-level Docker socket mount or a DinD sidecar) and the EPR range exposed on the node - plan node/host wiring before relying on it. Set NEKO_ROOMS_INSTANCE_URL/NAT1TO1 to your real public host. There is no built-in user database; protect the admin UI with NEKO_ROOMS_HTTP_AUTH or front it with an auth proxy and rotate any auth secret you set. No SMTP, S3 or external search engine is needed.
